title: Peltophorum rufum
intro: Peltophorum rufum, commonly known as the yellow flame tree or yellow ironwood, is a flowering plant species in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Peltophorum rufum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Peltophorum rufum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can be found in traditional remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions.
taxonomy: Peltophorum rufum is a member of the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Peltophorum is part of the tribe Phaseoleae. This classification indicates that Peltophorum rufum is a flowering plant related to other legumes. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
uses: Peltophorum rufum is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may be beneficial in treating various health conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.
